summary: The backend is built on a classic layered architecture using Spring Boot, Spring MVC, Spring Data JPA, Spring Security and Scheduler, exposing RESTful JSON APIs. It implements a comprehensive set of capabilities for legal deed archiving, handover management, key management and background job processing, while integrating with external key‑management services and a relational database. Although the system is functionally rich, the codebase shows moderate coupling and cohesion, a large number of controllers, services and entities, and signs of architectural debt that call for refactoring and stronger domain‑driven design discipline.

technical.integration_style: REST API (HTTP JSON) with internal service calls and scheduled jobs

capabilities: Create, read, update and delete deed entries
Track deed entry connections and logs
Handle handover data sets and history
Execute business actions and action streams
Perform archiving operations and sign submission/re‑encryption tokens
Manage encryption keys, re‑encryption and crypto state queries
Provide business purpose lookup and participant management
Generate reports and job status information
Schedule and run background jobs (e.g., re‑encryption, archiving)
integrations: External Key Management Service (via XnpKmServiceImpl / MockKmService)
External REST clients through ProxyRestTemplateConfiguration and TokenAuthenticationRestTemplateConfigurationSpringBoot

debt_indicators: Large number of controllers (32) some likely to be fat
Service layer dense (42 services) with many implementation classes
High entity count (199) suggests potential anemic domain model
Custom DAO implementations proliferating, indicating duplicated abstraction
Missing clear domain service interfaces for many business operations
recommendations: Introduce dedicated service interfaces and Facade layers to decouple controllers from concrete services
Refactor overly large controllers and services into smaller, task‑focused components
Consolidate related entities or apply aggregate roots to reduce entity explosion
Standardize repository abstractions; replace custom DAO implementations with reusable specifications or Spring Data extensions
Apply Domain‑Driven Design patterns (Aggregates, Domain Services) to improve cohesion within the domain layer
Add DTO mappings to separate API contracts from internal domain models, reducing coupling between layers

overall_assessment: The backend delivers a solid functional foundation and follows a clear layered pattern, but its moderate coupling, sprawling controller/service surface area, and anemic domain model raise maintainability concerns. Addressing the identified technical debt will improve scalability, testability and alignment with DDD principles.
top_recommendations: Introduce dedicated service interfaces and Facade layers to decouple controllers from concrete services
Refactor overly large controllers and services into smaller, task‑focused components
Consolidate related entities or apply aggregate roots to reduce entity explosion
